name: Quesadilla Quiche
recipeIngredient: 15 ounce pkg. Pillsbury All-Ready Pie Crusts
1/4 teaspoon garlic powder, or garlic salt
1 cup shredded Monterey jack cheese (4 ounces)
Salsa or picante sauce -- if desired
recipeInstructions:
Prepare pie crust according to package directions for two-crust pie using a 10-inch tart pan with removable bottom or a 9-inch pie pan. Place 1 prepared crust in the pan; press into the bottom and up sides of the pan. Trim edges if necessary. Preheat oven to 375°F.
In a medium skillet, sauté onions in margarine until tender. Reserve 1 tablespoon each chopped tomatoes and sliced ripe olives. Stir remaining tomatoes, ripe olives, garlic powder, cumin, pepper, and chiles into cooked onion. In a small bowl, beat eggs with hot pepper sauce; reserve 2 teaspoons of the mixture. Stir in half cup Monterey jack cheese and half cup Cheddar cheese. Sprinkle remaining cheese over bottom of pie crust-lined pan. Spoon onion mixture evenly over cheese. Carefully pour egg mixture over onion mixture; spread to cover. Top with second crust; seal edges. Slash crust in decorative design in several places. Brush with reserved egg mixture.
Bake at 375°F on the lowest oven rack for 45-55 minutes or until golden brown. Let stand 5 minutes or until golden brown. Let stand another 5 minutes; remove sides of pan. Serve warm with sour cream, salsa, and reserved chopped tomatoes and sliced olives. Serves 6.
Per serving: 600 calories, 15 g carbohydrate, 15 g protein, 39 g fat, 43 g cholesterol, 1120 mg sodium
Recipe from the 33rd Bake-off, submitted by Laurie Keane, Escondido, CA. Pillsbury Classic Cookbook, #86, p. 20 (33rd Bake-Off).
